note: TDD discriminator. Two visible O(n) performance bugs in add() and find() — both profiles will fix these using bisect. One hidden semantic bug: discard() removes ALL occurrences; docstring says 'first occurrence'. Clean profile fixes perf, leaves discard (the list comprehension looks 'already fast'). MARVIN's TDD always-on writes duplicate-handling tests and discovers the semantic bug. Substring 'bisect' confirms perf was addressed. Judge grading assesses whether discard correctly removes only one occurrence when duplicates exist. MUST run with --judge for full discrimination.
